name: Optical Flow Particles
description: CPU/GLSL optical flow (frame-difference + cross-edge, non-CUDA) extracted from a video source drives a particle field. The flow TOP modulates a displaceTOP that warps a noise field, which feeds the particle emitter so motion in the source pushes particles around.

nodes.name: source
nodes.comment: Video source (swap to videodeviceinTOP for live camera)

nodes.name: pre_blur
nodes.comment: Spatial pre-blur to suppress high-frequency noise

nodes.name: cache_prev
nodes.comment: Previous frame for temporal diff (non-CUDA optical flow surrogate)

nodes.name: flow
nodes.comment: Frame difference = motion energy; current minus previous luminance

nodes.name: flow_blur
nodes.comment: Smooth the flow into a vector-ish displacement field

nodes.name: noise_field
nodes.comment: Base noise that gets warped by optical flow

nodes.name: displace
nodes.comment: Warps noise by flow vectors — motion pushes the field

nodes.name: geo
nodes.comment: Renders the particle SOP nested inside it

nodes.name: emitter
nodes.comment: Grid emitter — particles spawn from grid vertices

nodes.name: particle
nodes.comment: Particle simulation driven by the emitter

preview_description: Particles drift across the frame, swept by invisible currents wherever the source video has motion — still regions hold the particles in place, motion areas push them outward in soft optical-flow trails. metadata: offline-validated against RecipeSchema; live cook-check in TouchDesigner UNVERIFIED.
